focus: Recurring content growth, repurposing, hook iteration, audience analytics, and monetization loops for creators.

candidate_requirements: Return 1-3 candidate domain chips only
Every candidate must describe a repeated specialization loop
Every candidate must describe a repeated mastery loop
Prefer domains with talkability, social spread, or obvious creator / builder pull
Reject personas, generic features, and vague workflow ideas

raw_candidates.label: Hook Angle Test Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Generates, tracks, and learns from repeated short-form hook variants so creators can systematically improve opening retention in their niche.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: testing and iterating opening hooks for short-form creator content across X, TikTok, Reels, and Shorts
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which curiosity gaps, claims, structures, and pacing patterns actually raise completion and hold rates for a creator's specific audience
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: draft hook variants, attach them to content drafts, compare early retention and completion outcomes, and update the creator's winning hook library after each post batch
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Recent creator-growth writing increasingly treats the first seconds of short-form content as an explicit testing surface. MRPOPULAR reports repeated hook testing as a core workflow, and Hook Studio argues completion-rate optimization has become the deciding growth loop rather than generic vanity metrics.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Creators are no longer just 'writing better hooks' ad hoc; they are repeatedly testing openings as a structured growth loop because the first seconds now decide whether content travels.

raw_candidates.label: Cross-Platform Clip Calendar Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Turns one long-form creator asset into a scheduled, cross-platform clip and post calendar that keeps learning which formats and channels actually compound reach.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: repurposing long-form creator content into recurring short-form clips, posts, and publishing plans across multiple channels
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which source segments, packaging formats, and platform-specific edits consistently generate reuse and distribution gains for a creator business
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: ingest a podcast, video, or thread, cut candidate clips and post variants, distribute them across channels, and refine the repurposing playbook from performance outcomes
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Creator tooling in 2025 is converging on repurposing as a repeated workflow rather than a one-off editing task. Repurpose.io frames the main problem as turning one long-form asset into many channel-native outputs, while Athenic presents structured automation flows that drip one source asset across multiple platforms over time.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: A lot of creator growth now depends on whether one idea can be operationalized into a repeatable distribution calendar across formats, not just whether the original post was good.

raw_candidates.label: Creator Membership Flywheel Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Helps creators convert attention into recurring paid-community revenue by packaging offers, launch cadence, member touchpoints, and retention signals into one repeatable operating loop.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: turning creator audience engagement into paid memberships, community offers, and recurring revenue operations
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which offers, launch structures, pricing bands, and retention rituals actually move followers into long-lived paid communities
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: analyze audience demand signals, draft the membership offer and launch sequence, ship recurring member value touches, and refine conversion and retention plays after each cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Recent creator-economy reporting points toward memberships and community as the stable monetization center of gravity. Circle's 2025 and 2026 reporting emphasizes recurring revenue and owned community as the core creator business model, while IAB's ad-spend growth shows attention is still expanding even as creators look for more durable monetization layers.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: For a lot of creators, the real business question is no longer just how to get views; it is how to repeatedly turn attention into a membership or community flywheel with stable monthly revenue.

raw_candidates.label: Thumbnail Package Test Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Builds and tracks repeated title, thumbnail, and opening-package experiments so creators can learn which packaging combinations actually lift click-through and early watch performance.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: testing and iterating video packaging combinations for creator content before and after publish
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which thumbnail-title-opening combinations consistently improve click-through and early watch behavior for a creator's niche and audience
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: generate packaging variants, map them to a release, compare test outcomes, and update the creator's packaging playbook after each publish cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: YouTube now treats thumbnail A/B testing as a core creator workflow, and creator discussion shows the next operational pain is coordinated title-plus-thumbnail testing rather than ad hoc manual swapping. That makes packaging optimization look like a repeated system, not a single feature.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Creators increasingly win or lose on packaging, and once platforms normalize A/B testing, the valuable layer becomes the repeated operating loop that decides what to test and what to keep.

raw_candidates.label: Audience Comment Signal Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Clusters comments and reply patterns into recurring audience personas, objections, and follow-up content opportunities so creators can turn feedback into their next content loop.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: mining creator comments and replies for repeated audience signals that shape future content and community strategy
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which audience questions, identity signals, objections, and emotional reactions consistently predict the next high-performing content directions
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: ingest comments and replies, cluster them into actionable audience signals, draft the next content or community moves, and refine the audience map after each release cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Recent creator-research work treats comments as a rich repeated signal layer rather than a moderation afterthought. Proxona shows creators struggle to interpret audience feedback with current tools, while SimTube frames believable comment generation and audience simulation as useful for refining content before release.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Comments, replies, and community reactions are increasingly part of the content engine itself; creators want a system that turns that mess into repeatable next-post and next-offer decisions.

raw_candidates.label: Creator Brand Deal Fit Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Builds recurring brand-fit briefs, pitch angles, and campaign match recommendations so creators can turn inbound attention into higher-conviction sponsorship revenue.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: matching creator identity, audience, and content format to repeatable brand partnership opportunities
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which creator-brand combinations, packaging angles, and campaign structures actually convert into recurring sponsorship and commerce revenue
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: analyze the creator's content and audience, generate brand-fit briefs and outreach angles, track deal outcomes, and refine the sponsorship playbook after each partnership cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: YouTube's 2025 brand-partnership roadmap and CreatorIQ's 2025-2026 state-of-market report both point to creator content being extended beyond social posts into broader media and commerce programs. The repeated bottleneck is identifying the right partnerships and packaging creators for them in a scalable way.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: As creator revenue matures, a lot of the leverage shifts from simply getting noticed to repeatedly matching the right creator, format, and audience story to the right sponsor or commerce program.

raw_candidates.label: Creator Shopping Drop Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Plans recurring product tags, affiliate bundles, and merch drops so creators can turn content spikes into a repeatable shopping revenue system.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: operating creator-led shopping and affiliate drops across tagged videos, shorts, and storefront collections
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which product bundles, timing windows, and merchandising angles consistently convert creator attention into shopping revenue
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: map content themes to products, schedule tagged drops and collections, track shopping conversions, and refine the creator's commerce playbook after each campaign cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: YouTube is explicitly expanding shopping infrastructure for creators through affiliate hubs, collections, and tagged video workflows, and its 2025 shopping report positions creator-led commerce as a major behavior shift. That makes creator shopping look like a repeated operating system, not a one-off monetization feature.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: A lot of creator monetization is shifting toward repeatable shopping moments where the real leverage is deciding what to tag, bundle, and push each time attention spikes.

raw_candidates.label: Fan Community Prompt Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Designs recurring community prompts, polls, fan threads, and post-video activation loops so creators can convert passive viewers into an active discussion layer.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: running creator-community prompts and discussion loops that extend content into recurring fan participation
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which prompt formats, timing windows, and follow-up moves consistently turn viewers into replying, returning, and participating community members
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: draft post-video prompts and polls, run discussion sequences in community surfaces, track fan participation patterns, and refine the creator's community activation playbook after each release cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: YouTube's Communities rollout explicitly treats fan discussion as its own product surface rather than a side effect of comments, and the 2025 Creator Collective program frames community-building as part of creator growth. That suggests a repeated prompt-and-response loop worth treating as a domain chip.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: For a lot of creators, growth no longer stops at the upload; the next lever is repeatedly getting fans to participate in community prompts, not just watch and leave.

raw_candidates.label: Creator Collab Pipeline Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Builds a recurring pipeline of creator collaboration targets, pitch angles, and format briefs so creators can turn occasional collabs into a compounding growth loop.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: finding, packaging, and operationalizing creator-to-creator collaborations as a repeatable audience-growth channel
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which collaborator matches, format pairings, and outreach angles consistently produce reach expansion and audience crossover
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: identify adjacent creators, draft the collaboration concept and pitch, track collab performance, and refine the creator's collaboration playbook after each partnership cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: YouTube's Creator Collective program is built around creators growing through connection and knowledge-sharing, while collaboration spotlights like Steven He and Jeenie Weenie show how creator pairings create durable audience crossover. The operational gap is turning those occasional wins into a system.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Collabs are still one of the clearest social growth levers for creators, but most people run them opportunistically instead of as a repeatable pipeline with proven match logic.

raw_candidates.label: Trend Riff Response Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Helps creators turn fast-moving internet trends into repeatable format responses by deciding when to jump, what angle to take, and how to package the riff for their audience.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: converting emerging meme, format, and culture trends into creator-native response content before the attention window closes
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which trend archetypes, timing windows, and creator-specific angles consistently generate participation, reach, and audience crossover
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: detect a rising trend, map it to a creator-specific riff angle, ship the response format, and refine the creator's trend-response playbook from outcome data
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: YouTube's 2025 trend reporting shows meme and format participation increasingly works through collaborative riffing rather than isolated publishing. The official trends report explicitly frames creators and viewers as co-creating culture, which makes trend-response packaging look like a repeated domain loop instead of a generic inspiration task.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: A lot of breakout creator growth now comes from joining the right trend at the right moment with the right angle, and most creators still do that by instinct instead of with a repeatable operating system.

raw_candidates.label: Virtual Creator Studio Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Runs the recurring scripting, persona, asset, and community loops needed to operate a virtual creator or avatar-led media brand consistently across videos, streams, and shorts.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: operating virtual-creator publishing systems with stable character identity, content planning, and audience interaction loops
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which persona rules, asset workflows, and interaction formats consistently maintain audience connection for avatar-led creator brands
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: plan the character beat, script and stage the content, update reusable assets, collect audience feedback, and refine the virtual creator playbook after each release cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: YouTube's 2025 virtual-creator report presents avatar-led channels as a real and growing creator class rather than a novelty niche, and Made On YouTube continues to position new creation and business tools around evolving creator workflows. That makes virtual-creator operations a plausible domain chip with its own repeated system needs.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Virtual creators are now large enough that the operational problem is no longer just making an avatar; it is running a stable character-led media system that keeps publishing and connecting with fans.

raw_candidates.label: Interactive Format Lab Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Designs recurring interactive video mechanics, viewer-choice prompts, and experiment loops so creators can systematically build more participatory content formats.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: developing creator-native interactive video formats with game-like or viewer-participation mechanics
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which interaction rules, branching prompts, and format structures consistently deepen participation and repeat viewing
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: prototype an interactive format, publish and track participation behavior, compare viewer response to alternative mechanics, and evolve the creator's interactive format library over time
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: YouTube's June 2025 trends note highlights a new wave of interactive game-like videos where the viewer experience depends on input and navigation rather than passive watching, while Creator Collective programming reinforces experimentation and peer-driven format learning. That suggests interactive format development is becoming a repeated creator-growth surface.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: As creators search for edges beyond simple posting frequency, interactive formats are becoming a real growth lever, but the experimentation loop is still messy and largely manual.
